name: Alex Chen
current_role: Software Engineer (2 years at a mid-size SaaS company)
goal_raw: I want to become a product manager
context: I like my engineering work but I keep gravitating toward product decisions. I've been informally doing PM-adjacent work — writing specs, talking to customers, prioritizing bugs. I want to make the official switch but I don't know if I should do it internally or look externally. I don't have an MBA and I'm not sure if I need one.
time_available: about 1.5 hours per day on weekdays, more on weekends
constraints: Full-time job, no budget for expensive bootcamps (< $500 okay), want to make the switch within 8 months
mood_baseline: Motivated but anxious — feels behind compared to peers who already have PM titles
